arts.ttl: Chelsea 5-0 Morecambe: Match report & talking points from FA Cup trouncing
arts.sum: As Chelsea found out on Saturday after Morecambe's Harry Burgoyne crept off his line to save Nkunku's penalty, there was no VAR in use across the FA Cup third round . In a display which secures Chelsea's passage to the FA Cup fourth round, not many of the club's B-team stated a strong case to be promoted to the Premier League XI.

arts.ttl: Chelsea leapfrog Real Madrid in race to sign Arsenal 'target' who idolises Mikel Arteta signing
arts.sum: "Ideally, we keep Jorrel for a long time and he will play in the Champions League at Ajax next season. Speaking to Dutch newspaper Het Parool in December, the club's technical director conceded their versatile defender, who can play left-back and centre-back, will one day leave.

arts.ttl: Tosin Adarabioyo responds to what Chelsea fans were shouting at him in the 5-0 win vs Morecambe
arts.sum: The goalscoring heroics of Tosin Adarabioyo were perhaps the biggest surprise in Chelsea’s win against Morecambe this weekend. Chelsea secured an impressive 5-0 victory at...

arts.ttl: Chelsea 5-0 Morecambe: Player ratings as Tosin & Felix star in FA Cup rout
arts.sum: After a hardly convincing first half, Maresca made three changes at the break, introducing the in-form Jadon Sancho, Malo Gusto and Marc Cucurella. Tosin converted a second strike from outside the box before Joao Felix notched a brace of his own in little more than 100 seconds.

arts.ttl: Enzo Maresca praises ‘professional’ Chelsea after easing past Morecambe
arts.sum: The 5-0 win at Stamford Bridge came after the visitors held off the Premier League outfit until shortly before half-time.

arts.ttl: Enzo Maresca explains why Noni Madueke wasn't in the Chelsea squad to face Morecambe
arts.sum: Chelsea’s name will be in the draw for the FA Cup fourth round after Saturday’s convincing win over Morecambe. Like expected, Chelsea came through fairly...

arts.ttl: Chelsea outclass League Two strugglers Morecambe
arts.sum: Chelsea cruise into the fourth round of the FA Cup, with a win over League Two strugglers Morecambe at Stamford Bridge, with defender Tosin Adarabioyo scoring twice from outside the box.

arts.ttl: João Félix’s late double rounds off Chelsea’s 5-0 thrashing of Morecambe
arts.sum: The broad numbers suggest that Christopher Nkunku should be one of Chelsea’s regulars. The France striker has 13 goals in all competitions this season, a tally equalled only by Cole Palmer, and he was on the scoresheet again as Enzo Maresca’s second string cruised into the fourth round of the FA Cup with a 5-0 win over Morecambe. However, while there are few better finishers in Maresca’s squad than Nkunku, the likelihood is that he will be on the bench when Bournemouth visit Stamford Bridge in the Premier League on Tuesday. Ultimately the sense is that some goals mean more than others. What more can Nkunku do? The answer: show more than the ability to breach opponents sitting second bottom of League Two. arts.ttl: Chelsea made to battle before easing past Morecambe into FA Cup fourth round
arts.sum: Tosin Adarabioyo and Joao Felix each scored twice for the Blues.
